event: Death of Saint Francis Xavier at Sancian
symbols: crucifix clutched by the dying saint — faith in Christ's redemptive death as the anchor of hope at the hour of death
angels descending from parted clouds — the heavenly reception of a faithful servant
palm branch borne by an angel — the martyr's or saint's victory over death and earthly suffering
heavenly golden light breaking through — divine glory welcoming the soul into paradise
black Jesuit robes — Francis Xavier's vocation and missionary obedience unto death
description: A vertical Baroque composition depicting a man in black robes lying on the ground in a posture of death or near-death, his face turned upward with an expression of spiritual transport. He clutches a crucifix to his chest. Above him, the sky opens in a burst of golden radiance as numerous angels and winged putti descend through parting clouds. One angel carries a palm branch. The setting appears to be a remote, rugged landscape with hints of tropical vegetation.

_meta.title: St. Francis Xavier Dying at Sancian
